title: abutilon sandwicense
intro: Abutilon sandwicense, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands and other parts of the Pacific. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Abutilon sandwicense include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Abutilon sandwicense can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions for topical use, as well as being used in tinctures for internal consumption.
